success_surface: Cell-level formula correction with circular reference diagnosis and resolution
effective_behaviors: Diagnosed formula logic before applying changes by inspecting original formula string and analyzing referenced range contents
Detected self-referential circular reference by verifying target cell position falls within its own formula's range bounds
Adjusted aggregation formula range to exclude the target cell while preserving calculation intent
Validated fix through independent calculation check before and after file save
Used separate input/output paths with directory creation to ensure proper file integrity
Reloaded saved workbook post-write to confirm formula strings persisted without conversion
evidence: Agent inspected cell B323 formula value directly: =IF(A323="E","",MAX(B$7:B377)+1)
Agent verified row 323 lies within range B7:B377 causing circular reference
Agent changed range from B$7:B377 to B$7:B322 to exclude self-reference
Agent independently calculated expected MAX value (605) + 1 = 606 before applying fix
Agent saved to separate output_path with os.makedirs() ensuring directory exists
Agent reloaded saved workbook and verified formula string persisted exactly as written

memory_items.title: Self-Reference Detection in Formula Ranges
memory_items.lesson: When modifying a cell's formula, verify the target cell's position does not fall within any aggregating function's range on the same column
memory_items.generalization: For cells containing MAX/SUM/AVERAGE/COUNT functions spanning a range, check if target_cell_row >= range_start_row AND target_cell_row <= range_end_row before writing

memory_items.title: Formula String Preservation Through Load-Save Cycle
memory_items.lesson: Formulas written to openpyxl cells persist correctly when saving, but always reload and verify to catch unexpected conversions
memory_items.generalization: After saving a modified workbook, load it fresh and compare the formula attribute of modified cells against intended strings

memory_items.title: Independent Result Validation Before Commitment
memory_items.lesson: Calculate expected output values using programmatic checks before finalizing formula changes to prevent silent errors
memory_items.generalization: For numeric aggregation fixes, pre-compute the expected result from the proposed range to verify the formula will produce correct output

edits.content:
## 6. Formula Safety & Validation
- **Self-Reference Check**: Before writing an aggregation formula (MAX/SUM/AVERAGE/COUNT), verify the target cell's row/column does not fall within the formula's own range. Adjust end points to exclude the target cell if necessary.
- **Range Boundary Verification**: Confirm that dynamic ranges or fixed ranges do not inadvertently include the cell being modified, especially for cumulative calculations.
- **Post-Save Formula Integrity**: After saving modifications, reload the workbook and verify the formula string persists unchanged (formulas should appear with leading '=' sign).
- **Pre-Write Calculation Check**: Independently calculate the expected result from the proposed formula range to validate correctness before committing changes.
